mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS path lowers discount rates and signals easier financial conditions, which lifts equity multiples and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior 3 months, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL acceleration is high while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real rates stay restrictive and inflation pressure compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 4% and FEDFUNDS stays above 5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the interaction term loses sign consistency across two consecutive rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above recent levels indicates weakening labor demand and higher recession probability, which depresses cyclicals and broad index returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points versus 3 months ago
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of UNRATE rise episodes, or if the signal's rolling 3-year information coefficient is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS reflects stronger nominal consumption and revenue growth, which supports earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive changes in RSAFS are not followed by above-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO improves while CPIAUCSL softens, firms experience stronger output with less input-cost pressure, expanding margins and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is below 3%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations fail to show positive excess 60-day SPX returns versus unconditional baseline, or if the joint signal turns negative in any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and broader cyclical slowdown, which usually precedes lower equity market returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not negative on average after 3-month HOUST declines, or if the effect reverses sign in more than one rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a payroll deceleration regime, a simultaneous drop in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE confirms labor market deterioration and should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change turns negative while UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE trigger does not outperform the single-series components, or if triggered 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 alongside firm PAYEMS indicates real activity is reaccelerating, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP upswings with payroll gains do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction signal's sign is unstable across two successive expansions.
